title: 24:36 ГўВҖВ” September 23, 2024
body_text:
24:36 ГўВҖВ” September 23, 2024
Update to the Enterprise Fraud Management Part
This update to the EFM Part is related to the recently released
news update
regarding the process change for Auto Risk flagged wires. In response to the feedback Central 1 received regarding Auto Risk wires and its related processes, subsequently sent wire transactions are no longer treated as Auto Risk transactions.
If thereГўВҖВҷs an alerted wire that has yet to be adjudicated, subsequently sent wire transactions are alerted and held until theyГўВҖВҷve been reviewed. The alerted wire is released or cancelled based on a fraud analystГўВҖВҷs adjudication. Internal procedures must be followed for the management of cancelled wires, including settlement to the customerГўВҖВҷs account.
Electronic Transactions Volume
Enterprise Fraud Management Part
Section 8.4, Identifying Alerts in Auto Risk Transactions
We added an important note to this section stating Auto Risk flags are only applicable to
Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
transactions.
Section 8.7, Wires in EFM and the Wires Application
We removed any references and explanations related to Auto Risk wires. A new scenario, ГўВҖВңSubsequently Alerted WireГўВҖВқ, was added. If there is an alerted wire that hasnГўВҖВҷt been adjudicated yet, the subsequentl) wire(s) sent after it is alerted and held. The wire(s) is held untila fraud analyst adjudicates it. Additionally, these alerted wires arenГўВҖВҷt cancelled automatically like they were in their previous state. These wires have a Pending status in the Wires application until theyГўВҖВҷre adjudicated by fraud analysts as ГўВҖВңRiskГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңNo-RiskГўВҖВқ. The management of cancelled wires and their settlement continue to follow your internal procedures.
